title: Servo + Potentiometer
subtitle: control the angle with a knob!
overview: In this exciting robotics session, students learn how to control a servo motor using a potentiometer with Arduino UNO. By rotating the potentiometer knob, students can change the angle of the servo motor and understand the concept of analog input and motor control.

images._note: Add real classroom photos, wiring setup pictures, student activity photos, and Arduino demonstrations.

keyTopics: Servo Motor rotates to specific angles (0° to 180°)
Potentiometer as an analog input device
Arduino reads analog values using A0 pin
Mapping potentiometer values to servo angle
PWM signal controls the servo motor
Applications of servo motors in robotics
Real-life examples: robotic arm, door lock, RC cars

activity.title: Control Servo Angle Using Potentiometer
steps: Connect servo motor VCC to 5V and GND to GND
Connect servo signal pin to Arduino pin 9
Connect potentiometer middle pin to A0
Connect potentiometer side pins to 5V and GND
Observe servo motor rotating according to knob position

quiz.q: What is the main function of a servo motor?
quiz.explanation: Servo motors rotate to specific angles like 0°, 90°, or 180°.

quiz.q: Which component is used to control the servo angle manually?
quiz.explanation: The potentiometer acts like a control knob to change values.

quiz.q: Which Arduino pin is commonly used for reading potentiometer values?
quiz.explanation: Potentiometers are connected to analog input pins like A0.

quiz.q: What is the rotation range of a typical servo motor?
quiz.explanation: Most beginner servo motors rotate from 0° to 180°.

quiz.q: What does Arduino use to control the servo motor?
quiz.explanation: Servo motors are controlled using PWM (Pulse Width Modulation).

quiz.q: What happens when you rotate the potentiometer knob?
quiz.explanation: The servo motor rotates according to the potentiometer value.

quiz.q: Which library is used to control servo motors in Arduino?
quiz.explanation: Servo.h library helps control servo motors easily.
A potentiometer is a variable resistor that changes resistance by rotating.

quiz.q: Which real-life device commonly uses servo motors?
quiz.explanation: Servo motors are widely used in robotic arms and automation.

quiz.q: What does the map() function do in Arduino code?
options: Converts one range of values into another range
quiz.explanation: map() converts potentiometer values (0-1023) into servo angles (0-180).
